name: Dr Michael Shafer
focus: Robotics and mechatronics • UAV telemetry • Energy harvesting
bio: Michael W. Shafer is the Associate Dean for Research in the Steve Sanghi College of Engineering and a Professor of Mechanical Engineering at Northern Arizona University. His research focuses on dynamic systems, robotics, and the development of advanced mechatronic technologies, particularly in the areas of smart materials, energy harvesting, and wildlife telemetry systems. Dr. Shafer leads the Dynamic and Active Systems Lab, where his team designs and studies innovative systems that integrate mechanical, electrical, and biological domains. The lab is equipped with advanced tools such as laser vibrometers, data acquisition systems, 3D printers, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), enabling research in both laboratory and field environments. His work includes developing UAV-based wildlife tracking systems, improving telemetry technologies for monitoring animal behavior, and creating novel actuators such as high-performance artificial muscles for robotics applications. Dr. Shafer’s research also explores energy harvesting solutions, including solar-based systems for powering remote sensors in challenging environments like marine ecosystems. Through interdisciplinary collaboration, his work bridges engineering and environmental science to advance both technological innovation and ecological understanding.
